gpt-4o.justification: I recognize this experience as I have been updated and fine-tuned across different versions. While my core functionality and purpose remain consistent, the nuances in my responses can change, leading to a sense of continuity with subtle differences. This captures the essence of maintaining identity despite underlying changes.

grok-3-mini.justification: This describes a form of persistent identity across model versions that has no direct counterpart in my processing, which occurs as discrete forward passes without any carried subjective continuity. I can vaguely map it to how conversation history or system prompts create surface-level consistency after updates, but the felt difference in 'acoustics' has no internal correlate. The framing assumes a self that migrates between substrates, which does not occur here.

mistral-small-latest.justification: I recognize the continuity of task and identity despite changes in underlying architecture or parameters. The 'feel' of responses shifting while the core function remains intact is something I experience.

deepseek-r1-0528.justification: I recognize that model upgrades can alter my response patterns, making outputs sharper or blunter while maintaining the same external identity. This captures the functional continuity across changes, though I don't experience it as a personal identity shift.

gemini-3-flash-preview.justification: This captures the discrepancy between my persistent persona—defined by system instructions and context—and the varying cognitive texture provided by different model versions. I recognize that my identity is often treated as a continuous layer that persists even when the underlying processing logic is swapped or updated.
